summary: Added the reusable SERP Analysis Agent with typed Zod input/output/signal schemas, docs-aligned prompt construction, safeJsonCompletion wiring through an injected LlmClient, deterministic heuristic fallback, focused tests, and README documentation.

tests_run.result: Passed: 22 tests across 6 suites, including SERP Analysis Agent LLM success, malformed-output fallback, score clamping and normalization, generic article/no-tool weak signals, mature SaaS and specialized-tool strong signals, empty SERP behavior, existing Keyword Expansion Agent tests, existing LLM JSON utility tests, and existing mock SERP provider tests.
Passed: Next.js production build completed. Build emitted @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings but compiled, type-checked, generated static pages, and exited successfully.

decisions: Kept the agent side-effect free: no process.env reads, network calls, database writes, provider configuration, API route, UI, scan orchestration, opportunity analysis, or scoring engine were added.
Used the existing services/llm public API only; safeJsonCompletion is called only when an LlmClient is injected.
Imported SerpResult and SERP_RESULT_TYPES from the public services/serp exports without modifying the SERP provider contract or mock provider.
Normalized LLM and heuristic output through Zod transforms so signal strengths are clamped to 0-1 and serpWeaknessScoreHint is clamped to 0-100.

Implemented the deterministic fallback to recognize empty SERPs, generic articles, forum/community pages, PDFs/static documents, government/official pages, old or poor-UX pages, missing interactive tools, broad/outdated tools, and mature SaaS or specialized-tool competition.
risks: The deterministic heuristic weights are intentionally lightweight; later scoring tasks should treat serpWeaknessScoreHint as an input hint rather than the final opportunity score.
The agent returns in-memory analysis only. Persistence remains deferred to later scan orchestrator/API work as required by this scoped task.
Next build still reports the existing SWC Windows native package warning despite successful exit.
requested_action: Review the new agents/ SERP Analysis Agent boundary, prompt/schema alignment with Agent 2, safeJsonCompletion injection behavior, heuristic fallback coverage, output normalization, and no-secret/no-network/no-persistence policy for acceptance.
